neighbors.name: C.W. Matthews Contracting Co., Inc.
neighbors.claims: C.W. Matthews Contracting Co., Inc. is a Georgia-based heavy highway construction company founded in 1946 that employs over 1,800 people statewide ([C.W. Matthews – History](https://cwmatthews.com/about/history/)). It remains held under the Matthews family legacy via its parent company, Matsco, Inc., and is headquartered in Marietta, Georgia ([C.W. Matthews – Leadership Team](https://cwmatthews.com/about/leadership/)). The company operates numerous asphalt manufacturing plants and terminals across Georgia, including a liquid asphalt storage terminal and plant in Rockmart, Polk County, which establishes its direct local presence ([C.W. Matthews – Asphalt Plants](https://cwmatthews.com/expertise/asphalt-plants-manufacturing/)). The company is a major federal and state contractor, though in 2015 it agreed to a settlement with the Department of Justice to resolve allegations related to compliance on federally funded highway construction projects ([Department of Justice](https://www.justice.gov/usao-ndga/pr/cw-matthews-agrees-pay-1-million-settle-allegations-it-violated-false-claims-act)). Locally, C.W. Matthews is engaged in the Polk County community, including hosting an annual Kids Fishing Event on company-owned property off Highway 113 ([Georgia Outdoor News](https://www.gon.com/news/page/95)). Furthermore, executive Michael Bell oversees all political, financial, and administrative activities for the firm ([C.W. Matthews – Leadership Team](https://cwmatthews.com/about/leadership/)).
neighbors.influence_justification: One of Georgia’s largest contractors (1,800+ employees); parent company executives oversee significant political activities; operates a local asphalt plant in Rockmart and hosts community events on company-owned property in Polk County.

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage CFO Michael Bell (who oversees external and political affairs) or President Dan Garcia directly to discuss the project. Emphasize potential subcontracting opportunities (e.g., road work, material supply) and reassure that the development will not impede their Rockmart plant operations.
